data.title: Genesis Energy launches $500M senior notes offering due 2034
data.description:
• Genesis Energy (GEL) on Wednesday said it has commenced a registered public offering of $500 million in aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be co-issued with Genesis Energy Finance Corporation and initially guaranteed by all subsidiaries other than unrestricted subsidiaries.
  • The company said it intends to use the net proceeds to purchase or redeem up to $490 million of its outstanding 7.75% senior notes due 2028, with any remaining proceeds to be used for general partnership purposes, including repaying borrowings under its senior secured credit facility.
  • GEL -1.92% premarket to $17.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Housing starts, building permits rise in December
data.description:
Housing starts rose 6.2% M/M to a 1.404M seasonally annual rate in December, compared with the prior month's revised 1.322M, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday.

Meanwhile, building permits were up 4.3% M/M to a 1.448M annual rate in December, compared to the revised 1.388M in November.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Tradr ETFs to launch leveraged ETFs linked to CLSK, COHR, LEU
data.description:
• Tradr ETFs said Wednesday it plans to launch three single-stock leveraged exchange-traded funds on Feb. 19, expanding its lineup of products aimed at active traders.
  • The CBOE-listed funds are designed to deliver either two times the inverse (-200%) or two times long (200%) the daily performance of a specific underlying stock, offering first-to-market leveraged exposure to each company.
  • The new ETFs include the Tradr 2X Short CLSK Daily ETF (CLSZ), linked to CleanSpark (CLSK); the Tradr 2X Long LEU Daily ETF (LEUX), tied to Centrus Energy (LEU); and the Tradr 2X Long COHR Daily ETF (COHX), linked to Coherent (COHR).
  • Year-to-date Price Performance: CLSK -8.3; LEU -17.8%; COHR +19.2%.

data.title: Trend Micro ADR reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Trend Micro ADR press release (TMICY): Q4 2025 Net income of $86M.
  • Revenue of $474M beats by $35.01M.
  • Operating income and net income attributable to owners of the parent company are expected to be 56,400 million Yen (or US $361 million) and 36,600 million Yen (or US $234 million), respectively.

data.title: Fusion Fuel to buy uranium royalty portfolio in $15M all-stock deal
data.description:
Fusion Fuel Green (HTOO) +11.8% pre-market Wednesday after saying it agreed to acquire a controlling interest in Royal Uranium Inc., a portfolio of uranium royalties spanning the exploration and development sectors of the mining cycle, in a deal that values the Canadian company at ~$15M.

Under the deal terms, Royal Uranium’s shareholders will exchange more than 78.5M common shares for up to ~3.75M Fusion Fuel (HTOO) shares or equivalent pre-funded warrants.

Fusion Fuel (HTOO) said the acquisition will bring a 75%-100% stake in Royal Uranium and exposure to a portfolio of 16 uranium and three natural gas royalties in the Americas "from some of the globe's most significant uranium miners operating projects located in world-class uranium districts."

"By agreeing to acquire exposure to uranium and clean energy royalties, we are entering an asset class that is experiencing strong structural growth and is increasingly central to global energy development," Fusion Fuel (HTOO) CEO JP Backwell said.

data.title: Medicare Advantage growth reportedly slows down as UnitedHealth, CVS retreat
data.description:
Americans enrolled in private Medicare Advantage plans continued to grow from 2025 to 2026 but at a slower pace as nearly all health insurers, including UnitedHealth (UNH) and CVS Health (CVS), pulled back from the market, Stat News reported, citing new federal data.

As of Feb. 1, approximately 35.5M Americans were enrolled in Medicare Advantage, a government-funded healthcare program that mainly caters to older adults and people with disabilities. That indicates nearly 3% growth from last year, when there were roughly 34.4M MA enrollments.

Historically, MA annual enrollment growth stood at 7%–10% between 2017 and 2024. Stat reported adding that the annual enrollment period, which runs from October 15 to December 7 each year, showed only a 1% increase in MA enrollments in 2025.

According to the analysis, nearly all health insurers cut their enrollments, while Humana (HUM) increased its numbers.

UnitedHealth (UNH) is the largest operator in the MA market, followed by Humana (HUM). CVS Health’s (CVS) Aetna unit, Clover Health (CLOV), and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC) are among other players in the MA market.

data.title: Greenlane appoints CEO
data.description:
Greenlane Holdings (GNLN) on Wednesday announced the appointment of Jason Hitchcock as CEO, effective immediately.

Shares were -5.97% pre-market to $0.81.

Most recently, Hitchcock served as the head of business development at thirdweb, a leading open-source Web3 developer tools and infrastructure platform.

As CEO, Hitchcock will oversee corporate strategy, capital allocation, operational execution, and the continued development of Greenlane's digital asset treasury initiatives.

data.title: Cadence Design Systems rises as Wall Street praises results, guidance
data.description:
Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) was in focus on Wednesday after Wall Street praised the electronic design automation firm's stronger-than-expected results and guidance.

Shares rose nearly 7% in premarket trading, while competitor Synopsys (SNPS) rose 3.5%.

"CDNS remains our top EDA pick as their portfolio is seeing share gains across all segments with 1) ASIC-based hardware platform remaining best-in-breed, 2) stronger exposure to TSMC ecosystem and at new foundries translating into IP share gains, and 3) 3DIC (~50% of SD&A) and physical AI (other ~50% of SD&A) EDA tools also taking share in expanding [total addressable markets]," Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya wrote in a note to clients. Arya reiterated his Buy rating on Cadence, but slightly lowered his price target to $375 from $400 after the results.

Wells Fargo analyst Joe Quatrochi was similarly impressed and said the strong guidance could wind up proving conservative.

"Cadence's initial 2026 guide of $5.90-$6.0B implies +12.3% y/y at the midpoint, which was inline with our previewed buyside bogey," Quatrochi wrote. "That said, w/ 1Q26 guide implied at +16% y/y (we believe HW-driven) & re-accelerating recurring [software] demand, we expect continual upside throughout 2026. We expect the company to highlight improving [hardware] demand as the yr. progresses given ~6 month visibility." Quatrochi reiterated his Outperform rating, but like Arya, slightly lowered his price target to $375 from $410.

Oppenheimer analyst Edward Yang was a bit more tempered in his take, as he believes the fourth-quarter revenue beat of 1% (below the typical 2%) did not "resolve the longer-term AI debate."

"The SD&A segment that correlates to Dassault's recent weakness was surprisingly down 11% yoy, and 2026 guidance was only in line with the consensus estimate (no raise), implying further revenue deceleration to ~12% from ~14%," Yang wrote in a note to clients. He maintained his Perform rating and $275 price target on the stock.

data.title: Wingstop rallies after same-store sales don't fall as much as anticipated
data.description:
Wingstop (WING) rallied in early trading on Wednesday after the restaurant company reported fourth-quarter earnings results.

System-wide sales increased 9.3% to $1.3 billion. Domestic same-store sales fell 5.8% during the quarter at company-owned stores, although that was better than the consensus estimate for a decline of 6.8%.

Cost of sales as a percentage of company-owned restaurant sales decreased to 75.6% from 77.6% in the prior fourth quarter. The decrease was primarily driven by sales leverage on other operating expenses, as well as a decline in food, beverage, and packaging costs primarily resulting from a decrease in the cost of bone-in chicken wings as compared to the prior fourth quarter.

Wingstop (WING) churned up adjusted EBITDA of $61.9M during the quarter vs. $56.5M a year ago.

As of December 27, there were 3,056 Wingstop restaurants system-wide. This included 2,586 restaurants in the United States, of which 2,529 were franchised restaurants and 57 were company-owned, and 470 franchised restaurants were in international markets, including U.S. territories. During the fourth quarter of 2025, there were 124 net system-wide Wingstop restaurant openings.

CEO update: "In a year marked by uncertainty, the structural advantages of our operating model are reflected in our 15% adjusted EBITDA growth in 2025. This year's performance reflects the compelling returns of our unit economics, but also the confidence in our strategy that will enable our vision of reaching more than 10,000 restaurants globally."

Looking ahead, Wingstop (WING) sees flat to low-single-digit domestic same-store sales growth. A global unit growth rate of 15% to 16% is anticipated.

Shares of Wingstop (WING) rallied 13.7% in premarket trading to $286.25 vs. the 52-week range of $204.00 to $388.14. Short interest on WING stands at 13.2% of the total float.

data.title: Moody's Q4 earnings beat as investors services revenue hits record
data.description:
Moody’s (MCO) stock rose 2.4% in Wednesday premarket trading after the company posted a hefty Q4 earnings beat and issued strong 2026 earnings guidance, as its investors services business turned in its best revenue growth for Q4 on record.

The ratings and market analytics company expects full-year 2026 adjusted EPS of $16.40-$17.00 (midpoint $16.70 vs. $16.45 consensus). Revenue is expected to increase at the high single-digit range compared with 9% growth in 2025. The company projects free cash flow of $2.8B-$3.0B vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.99B.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $3.64, topping the average analyst estimate of $3.44, decreased from $3.92 in Q3 and increased from $2.62 in Q4 2024.

Q4 revenue of $1.89B, beating the $1.87B consensus, fell from $2.01B in the prior quarter and rose from $1.67B in the year-ago period. 

Analytics revenue of $943M climbed from $909M in Q3 and $863M in the prior year’s Q4. The division’s 9% Y/Y growth includes 12% growth in Decision Solutions, 6% in Research and Insights, and 8% in Data & Information. Recurring revenue, comprising 97% of Moody's Analytics revenue, rose 11% on a reported basis and 8% on an organic constant currency basis.

Investors Service revenue of $946M compared with $1.10B in the previous quarter and $809B a year ago. The 17% Y/Y growth marks its strongest Q4 on record. Investment-grade revenue surged 97% as issuance soared 127%; high-yield bonds revenue grew 9% Y/Y, and public, project, and infrastructure finance revenue climbed 30% as issuance increased 46%. Leveraged loans revenue declined 14% Y/Y, reflecting the record issuance levels of Q4 2024.

Expenses of $1.12B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.10B, increased from $1.09B in Q3 and $1.11B in Q4 2024.

Adjusted operating margin of 48.7% slipped from 52.9% in the prior quarter and increased from 43.8% a year ago.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.
